title: Folliculitis
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives.
supports: Folliculitis is inflammation or infection of one or more hair follicles.
Common symptoms include rash, itching, and pimples or pustules near hair follicles.
Folliculitis can occur after follicle damage or blockage, including from rubbing or shaving.
Patients should seek care for spreading infection, recurrent disease, or higher-risk presentations.
